rulings.Case Title: PETER ENGLANDER VS. PULL'R HOLDING COMPANY, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment As To Defendant Pull'R Holding Company, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 15, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PETER ENGLANDER's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment As To Defendant Pull'R Holding Company, Llc. Plaintiff Peter Englander's motion to approve its Proposition 65 settlement with defendant Pull'R Holding Company, LLC is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Englander is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed consent judgment signed by all parties and must bring the proposed order and judgment to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDY PUBILL ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 15, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the ninth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: KONSTANZE VON SEEFRIED ET AL VS. MOHANAD ABABNEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 15, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT MOHANAD ABABNEH, FERAS ALSHRAIDEH, NOOR EISSA's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Defendants Mohanad Ababneh, Feras Alshraideh and Noor Eissa's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. No opposition filed. The cross-complaint must be filed by August 24, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving parties is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
